Fix cycle: initial verification (2026-07-05-001-verification) returned CHANGES_REQUESTED on a genuine TOCTOU race in NewSnapshot (epoch acquired after reading CurrentVersion, allowing premature reclamation of a version a live snapshot still referenced). Fixed in 2026-07-05-002-implementation-fix (commits acc7601/afb3fbc): reordered NewSnapshot to acquire the epoch first, added regression test TestNewSnapshotClosesEpochAcquireVersionReadRace. Re-verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS in 2026-07-05-003-verification: happens-before proof independently re-derived against actual mutexes, race-free, zero regressions, 20x -race clean. Non-blocking: fix trades a slightly wider conservative-retention window for correctness.

Fix cycle: 006-verification returned CHANGES_REQUESTED (doc comment overclaimed equivalence to 2a.2.2's deterministic TOCTOU regression test -- disproved by a 30-run revert-and-rerun experiment showing zero detections; and readers using a fixed round count finished in ~7% of testDuration instead of overlapping writers/compactor throughout). Fixed in 007-implementation-fix (commit a1f220d): corrected doc comment to state this test complements but does not substitute for the deterministic regression test, and changed readers to loop on the shared stop signal for the full test duration with an in-test overlap assertion. Re-verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS in 008-verification: readers now overlap ~100% of test duration (vs ~7% before), assertion correctly wired, zero regressions, clean at 10x/20x -race. Test-only fix cycle; no production code touched. Last of task-2a.2's 3 subtasks; task-2a.2 and issue #7 now closable.

Supersedes earlier planned entry. All 3 subtasks now verified: 2a.2.1 (EpochManager primitive, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS), 2a.2.2 (live snapshot/commit wiring, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS after one fix cycle), 2a.2.3 (GC concurrency stress test, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS after one test-only fix cycle). Added TestStripedConcurrencyStress: 2000 fileIDs, one goroutine each, 5 fixed Put/Delete patterns keyed by fileID%5, oracle derived statically per pattern; -race clean, full package no regressions, stable across -count=3.

Added BenchmarkStripedVsGlobalLock (engine/catalog/catalog_bench_test.go): Striped/GlobalLock sub-benchmarks over a minimal in-memory harness (reuses real stripeFor(fileID) + CatalogRecord.Encode/Decode) differing only in lock granularity (256 stripes vs 1 global mutex); real disk-backed Catalog/FileManager wrapping was tried first but pread/pwrite syscall cost masked the lock-contention signal. Measured: Striped ~230 ns/op vs GlobalLock ~454 ns/op (~2x), consistent across repeated runs; independently re-confirmed 3x during verification. -race -v -count=1 clean, no regressions, build/vet/gofmt clean. Last subtask of task-2a.3 (issue #8); parent task and issue now closable.

Supersedes earlier planned entry. Both subtasks now verified: 2a.3.1 (concurrent stress test across fileIDs verifying stripe isolation, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS) and 2a.3.2 (contention benchmark: striped mutex vs global-lock baseline, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS). Closes issue #8 (Catalog concurrent-correctness hardening) in full.

1 of 5 subtasks under task-2a.4 (issue #9, latch-crabbing B+Tree concurrency), the last task of Phase 2a. Added engine/btree/latch.go: NodeStore-owned map[uint64]*nodeLatch registry (plain sync.Mutex for writer-only crabbing, atomic.Uint64 version counter, single-increment-after-mutation scheme). WriteNode bumps version by exactly 1 per successful write but does not itself acquire the latch (documented convention: 2a.4.2/2a.4.3 crabbing call sites must Lock/Unlock around WriteNode). insert.go/delete.go call sites intentionally left unwired. Verification confirmed lazy-population race is NOT present, WriteNode is the sole mutation choke-point, and the 50-goroutine concurrent test is genuine. Two non-blocking findings (registry has no eviction; node.go's old doc comments are now stale) tracked in .cdr/memory/pending.md. Parent task-2a.4 stays planned; subtasks 2a.4.2-2a.4.5 pending.

Round 3 (fourth agent dispatch): orchestrator personally caught the round-3 stress test (160g/80k) genuinely DEADLOCKED for 40+ minutes (goroutine dump: scratchpad/goroutine_dump_deadlock.log) -- multiple goroutines permanently blocked on sync.Mutex.Lock inside NodeStore.Lock, called from Tree.findParent/Tree.propagate, each waiting on a latch held by another blocked goroutine: a genuine lock-ordering CYCLE, not just a data race, and very likely the same underlying defect behind 024-verification's unconfirmed silent-data-loss symptom. Root cause confirmed: crabInsert/findParent's hand-over-hand ('lock next, release current') descent used a blocking Lock for every step; because this tree's design lets a node be reached both via its parent's already-linked Children entry AND via a not-yet-linked sibling's NextLeaf/NextSibling pointer at the same time, two concurrent crabbing walks could end up each holding a latch the other needed. Structural fix: every hand-over-hand step in crabInsert/findParent now uses NodeStore.TryLock (new method, latch.go); on a miss, the walk releases everything it holds and restarts from the root (errRestartFromRoot, insert.go) after a small jittered backoff, making a lock-ordering cycle structurally impossible by construction (no goroutine can ever block on a latch while holding a different one). Added TestCrabbingConcurrentPropagateNoDeadlock (insert_test.go): a fast (<0.1s), deterministic regression test using a synchronization hook (crabRetryHook), in the same spirit as engine/mvcc's hook-based pause pattern, to force the exact TryLock-collision interleaving without relying on large-scale random contention. Re-ran the 160g/80k TestCrabbingInsert suite plus the new regression test at -count=10 -race -timeout 5m (165s, all pass, no hang) and the full engine module at -race -timeout 5m (all pass). Also confirmed (again) that round 2's sort.Search fix was a no-op equivalent to the pre-fix 'pos := j' formula; reverted to the simpler form. Cleaned up all round-3 temporary diagnostics (debugWriteHook, zzrepro_instrumented_test.go); kept the O(1) promoted-key bounds check in propagate as a permanent invariant. Superseded the abandoned 2026-07-05/025-implementation-fix run (cut off mid-experiment, never reached implementation) with 2026-07-06/001-implementation-fix. Does NOT independently re-confirm the original silent-data-loss symptom is fixed by this specific change (plausible given the shared mechanism, but not separately proven) -- flagging for /cdr:verify. VERIFIED (2026-07-06-002-verification, 4th pass): deadlock structurally eliminated (30 additional clean repro runs at 160g/80k), silent-data-loss symptom not reproduced in 30 own runs (favorable, not airtight). Subtask closed; GitHub issue #9 resolved. Two low-risk non-blocking items carried forward (no retry-attempt metric; data-loss absence not mathematically proven). [2a.4.5 cross-cutting note]: task-2a.4.5's fix cycle (commit b31328f) found that this task's leaf-sibling move-right assumption (a NextLeaf sibling is always a non-empty, just-created split right-half) was correct in isolation but became unsafe once 2a.4.3's tombstone/empty-leaf policy was added; also found that this task's leaf/internal split write order was correct for crabbing readers but unsafe for 2a.4.4's later lock-free Tree.Lookup. Both were fixed in 2a.4.5, not here; no change needed to this task's own commit.

3 of 5 subtasks under task-2a.4 (issue #9, latch-crabbing B+Tree concurrency). Concurrent Tree.Delete via latch-crabbing, reusing 2a.4.2's TryLock + full-release + restart-from-root discipline unmodified. One fix round: 005-verification returned CHANGES_REQUESTED because findLeftNeighborAtSameLevel's dangling-NextSibling repair after an ancestor splice only handled the same-grandparent (gj > 0) case; whenever the spliced ancestor was its grandparent's first child (gj == 0), the true left neighbor under an adjacent grandparent was deterministically never patched, a standing corruption risk since NextSibling is load-bearing for move-right routing during live descent. 006-implementation-fix (f949d54) generalized the repair to a walk-up-then-descend-down algorithm correct at any nesting depth, and fixed the regression test to exercise the concurrent Tree.Delete path (it had previously called the single-threaded free-function Delete and never reached the code under test). 007-verification returned PASS, with one non-blocking test_coverage comment: the multi-level-nested gj==0 case was verified only via a temporary, non-committed test; a committed regression test for that case is recommended follow-up. See .cdr/commits/task-2a.4.3.md for full record. [2a.4.5 cross-cutting note]: task-2a.4.5's fix cycle (commit b31328f) found that this task's leaf-sibling move-right assumption (a NextLeaf sibling is always a non-empty, just-created split right-half) was correct in isolation but became unsafe once 2a.4.3's tombstone/empty-leaf policy was added; also found that this task's leaf/internal split write order was correct for crabbing readers but unsafe for 2a.4.4's later lock-free Tree.Lookup. Both were fixed in 2a.4.5, not here; no change needed to this task's own commit.

Tree.Lookup: lock-free optimistic version-counter read, never calls Lock/TryLock on per-node latches; version-before/ReadNode/version-after per node incl. Blink-tree move-right siblings; mismatch restarts whole lookup from root (no cap, mirrors crabInsert/crabDelete). Torn-read residual risk (single 4096B ReadAt/WriteAt page-atomicity assumption, not a POSIX guarantee) confirmed sound and documented as deferred, non-blocking. F1 (non-blocking): retry loop's t.Root() briefly acquires rootMu, narrowly contradicting doc comments' literal 'never calls Lock/TryLock' claim; tracked in .cdr/memory/pending.md.

Blocker fix cycle (commit b31328f, docs follow-ups 2644ac6/9cd91f6) independently verified in 014-verification: PASS_WITH_COMMENTS, all three root causes re-derived and judged structurally correct, acceptance tests independently re-run (TestConcurrentMixedWorkload -race -count=5, 5/5 clean; full engine/btree and whole-module suites green under -race, zero regressions). task-2a.4's shared insert/delete/lookup surface judged 'closable' -- no further residual cross-cutting gap found. Non-blocking follow-ups tracked, not blocking: (a) bugs 2/3 lack dedicated fast regression tests of their own (only probabilistic capstone coverage); (b) pre-existing no-retry-cap TryLock restart-from-root loop has fresh evidence of multi-minute non-terminating churn at large scale under a plain (non-race) build, reinforcing existing .cdr/memory/pending.md entry. State remains 'implemented' (not 'verified'): this fix-cycle verification covers only the cross-cutting bugs it introduced/fixed, not 2a.4.5's own subtask-level implementation, which still needs its own commit + re-run of TestConcurrentMixedWorkload against this fix + its own self-consistency + its own handoff before /cdr:verify can close 2a.4.5 itself. 5 of 5 subtasks under task-2a.4 (issue #9, latch-crabbing B+Tree concurrency). 012-implementation's capstone TestConcurrentMixedWorkload discovered a genuine, reliably-reproducing concurrency bug via a minimal 20-goroutine insert-only/delete-only-on-disjoint-ranges repro (documented in 012's failure.json): silent data loss and, in other interleavings, propagate's own invariant panic. 013-implementation-fix root-caused and fixed THREE independent bugs, all interactions between 2a.4.2's insert-only-era assumptions and 2a.4.3/2a.4.4's later additions: (1) crabInsertOnce/crabDeleteOnce/Tree.Lookup's leaf-level move-right peek mishandled an empty NextLeaf sibling (safe pre-2a.4.3 tombstone policy, unsafe after); (2) repairEmptyLeafAtParent's borrow/merge-from-left (delete.go) could orphan a concurrently-split-but-not-yet-propagated left-sibling successor node; (3) leaf/internal split write order (insert.go) let Tree.Lookup's lock-free optimistic reads (2a.4.4) observe a pointer to a not-yet-written node (EOF errors). Also relaxed assertStructuralInvariants' (insert_test.go) LowKey check from exact-equality to never-exceeds, matching InternalNode.LowKey's documented fixed-at-creation-only contract (test-helper fix, not production). TestConcurrentMixedWorkload's scale reduced (80k keys/200 goroutines -> 8k keys/50 goroutines) after finding the original scale hits a separate, pre-existing retry-backoff throughput/contention ceiling under -timeout=20m even alone with no other load (confirmed NOT a correctness regression: original scale passes cleanly under -race, both single-run and -count=3); this scale-sensitivity itself was not root-caused, only worked around, and is flagged for /cdr:verify and any future scale-focused subtask. Added TestConcurrentInsertDeleteDisjointRangesMinimalRepro as a fast committed regression test for bug 1 (validated -count=200 plain, -count=100 -race). Full existing suite plus both capstone tests pass under -race with zero regressions. See also cross-cutting interaction notes added to task-2a.4.2 and task-2a.4.3 entries.

Second of 3 subtasks under task-2b.1 (issue #10); 2b.1.3 (catalog SPLITTING status transition) is the last remaining subtask. Added engine/split/guard.go: FileGuard, per-fileID atomic-CAS-backed splitInProgress flag ensuring exactly one split wins per threshold crossing; lazy map-creation path guarded by mutex only (not steady-state CAS ops), matching engine/btree/latch.go's NodeStore precedent. TestSplitInProgressCAS: many goroutines race CAS on same fileID, exactly one winner asserted via atomic counter. VERIFIED (2026-07-07-005-verification): PASS, zero findings, high confidence. Verifier explicitly traced CAS correctness end-to-end: no plain-mutex+bool TOCTOU bug, no double-map-entry/lost-update bug in lazy map-creation path. go test ./split/... -race -run TestSplitInProgressCAS -count=100 and full -race -count=1 suite both green; build/vet/gofmt clean. Non-blocking: FileGuard's per-fileID registry has same deliberate no-eviction growth characteristic as engine/btree/latch.go's NodeStore (task-2a.4.1/issue #9 precedent); tracked in .cdr/memory/pending.md (line 9). Commit: bed7edd (feat).

Third and last of 3 subtasks under task-2b.1 (issue #10); issue #10 is now fully closed. Added engine/split/orchestrate.go: Orchestrator composing 2b.1.2's FileGuard CAS guard with CatalogRecord.Status transitions - BeginSplit (guard.TryAcquire -> transitionStatus to StatusSplitting, WAL-before-apply), EndSplit/AbortSplit (validated exit transition, unconditional guard release via deferred cleanup), AdmitWrite (write-admission gate refusing new writers while SPLITTING). Reader isolation proven directly: reader_snapshot_unaffected_by_splitting takes a real mvcc.NewSnapshot before BeginSplit and asserts byte-identical reads during/after the split window; independently confirmed engine/mvcc has zero references to CatalogRecord.Status. concurrent_writers_and_split_race races 32 goroutines' AdmitWrite against BeginSplit/AbortSplit under -race, asserting both admitted>0 and refused>0. VERIFIED (2026-07-07-008-verification): PASS_WITH_COMMENTS, zero must-fix, one should-fix (mirror the abandoned-SPLITTING-record gap into pending.md, done during verification). Non-blocking, honestly-disclosed gap: abandoned SPLITTING record on split-holder crash has no automatic recovery (no timeout/repair path); deferred because no split executor exists yet (issue #12 owns that); tracked in .cdr/memory/pending.md. engine/split/guard.go and trigger.go confirmed byte-identical to prior verified commit bed7edd (untouched). Commit: 124490b (feat).

All 3 subtasks (2b.1.1 threshold detection commit 9214a83, 2b.1.2 CAS guard commit bed7edd, 2b.1.3 SPLITTING orchestration commit 124490b) implemented and independently verified. Issue #10 closable. Two non-blocking follow-ups tracked in .cdr/memory/pending.md for issue #12: (a) two independent, currently-matching threshold-crossing implementations (engine/split/trigger.go vs engine/catalog/content.go's stub) with no drift-detection test; (b) abandoned SPLITTING record on split-holder crash has no automatic recovery. Both subtasks (2b.2.1 SplitProposer interface commit 02b6e2d, 2b.2.2 MockSplitProposer commits 3c83d72+f8abd00) implemented and independently verified. Issue #11 closable. First of 6 subtasks under task-2b.3 (issue #12, Atomic split-transaction execution). Added engine/split/execute.go: ExecuteSplitAllocateAndWrite validates a SplitPlan, allocates exactly one new fileID per SplitFileProposal via catalog.IDAllocator.Next() (shared allocator, no separate ID space), and writes the new content file via a temp-file+rename durable-write sequence mirroring catalog.ContentStore.writeContentFile byte-for-byte, using cs.ContentPath(newFileID). Deliberately does not touch catalog/B+Tree/graph and provides no cross-step atomicity (deferred to 2b.3.6). VERIFIED (2026-07-07-016-verification): PASS_WITH_COMMENTS. go test ./split/... -race -v -count=1 and ./catalog/... -count=1 green, TestSplitAllocateAndWrite -count=5 stable. All 9 subtests confirmed non-vacuous. Non-blocking, carried forward: no test for a zero-length range touching another non-zero range boundary across two proposals; extractSections has no defensive re-check if reused outside the validated call path. Commits: 01411d8 (feat), a7c3c74 (chore/metadata). Issue #12 has 5 subtasks remaining (2b.3.2-2b.3.6); not pushed.

Subtask 2/6 under issue #12. 4 non-blocking comments carried forward: (1) wrong_status/record_not_found subtests assert only returned error not explicit zero-side-effects, (2) reused-fileID check-Status-before-trusting-content invariant is doc-only, (3) stub-content determinism depends on caller-supplied newFileIDs ordering -- tracked in pending.md for 2b.3.6, (4) non-atomic stub-write/catalog-transition window disclosed and recoverable, acceptable given 2b.3.6 owns atomicity.

Subtask 3/6 issue #12. ExecuteSplitBtreeInsert: new-path insertion + old-path repoint as safe no-op via btree.Tree.Insert upsert semantics. Non-blocking: (1) raw path strings used as btree keys, no normalization/namespace layer, tracked in pending.md; (2) no subtest for oldPath absent pre-call, code analysis shows it works as fresh insert.

Subtask 4/6 under issue #12. New engine/graph package: minimal append-only Edge writer for SPLIT_SIBLING/REDIRECT edges, reusing engine/wal low-level segment writer without the TypedRecord/Replay recovery layer. Non-blocking, carried forward: (1) MOST IMPORTANT -- edge-append records are fsynced/durable but NOT integrated into any crash-recovery replay path (unlike catalog/btree via wal.Replay); must be explicitly resolved by 2b.3.5 or 2b.3.6, e.g. folding graph writes into 2b.3.6 WAL-covered transaction. Tracked as a flagged, non-deferrable item in pending.md. (2) AppendEdge rejects invalid EdgeType but not Source/Target==0; revisit at 2b.3.5 call site.

ExecuteSplitGraphEdges: SPLIT_SIBLING complete-directed-graph among new files + EdgeRedirect from originalFileID to each new fileID; pre-existing inbound edges to originalFileID require zero mutation since 2b.3.2 reuses that fileID as redirect stub. Subtask 5/6 under issue #12. Crash-recovery gap deliberately deferred to 2b.3.6 per its acceptance criteria. Non-blocking: inbound-edge test proves log-presence only (no query API yet); O(N^2) sibling topology on unconsumed log, revisit post-Epic-3.

Subtask 6/6 (FINAL) under issue #12. ExecuteSplitAtomic: new wal.RecordSplitCommit wraps catalog Put + btree insert/repoint + graph edge appends inside one wal.AppendAndApply closure; RecoverSplitCommits idempotent replay; AppendEdgeIfAbsent idempotent graph replay. Verified NOT to reintroduce non-atomic window: re-implements 2b.3.2/2b.3.3/2b.3.5's logic inline rather than composing their functions; repo-wide grep confirms zero production call sites compose them together. Non-blocking: (1) crash-injection subtests replay against same in-memory objects rather than freshly reconstructed ones, (2) cosmetic duplicated/orphaned AppendAndApply doc-comment block in engine/wal/record.go (~lines 342-370), tracked in pending.md. Residual risks accurately disclosed: btree SaveRoot/WAL-replay gap untouched (pre-existing); FileGuard abandoned-SPLITTING window narrowed (not eliminated). Issue #12 now closable: all 6 subtasks verified. Closure (push + gh issue close) deferred pending user push authorization, same pattern as issue #11.

All 6 subtasks (2b.3.1 allocate+write commit 01411d8, 2b.3.2 redirect stub commit 73279a0, 2b.3.3 btree repoint commit ee2a658, 2b.3.4 graph edge-append primitive commit 3cf7e2c, 2b.3.5 SPLIT_SIBLING/REDIRECT edges commit 8f8f85d, 2b.3.6 atomic WAL-covered commit commit be6523d) implemented and independently verified. Auto-split's full execution pipeline now implemented end-to-end and crash-safe via a single atomic WAL transaction. Issue #12 closable -- all 6 subtasks verified, zero must-fix findings. Closure deferred pending push authorization, same pattern as issue #11 (.cdr/commits/task-2b.2-issue-11.md).

Sole subtask under GitHub issue #13 (Section-index staleness invalidation, Epic Phase 2b). Adds per-file header-offset cache backing new ReadPartial, invalidated on append and split content-boundary changes. Initial impl (commit 4b8fe67) verified CHANGES_REQUESTED for a genuine split-path stripe-lock atomicity race (stale ReadPartial window between cat.Put and InvalidateHeaderCache); fix cycle (commit 9ba2f07) closed the race by sharing the append path's per-file lock and added a provably-effective -race concurrency test (confirmed catches the bug via revert-and-reproduce). Independent re-verification PASS. Closes issue #13 in full; GitHub closure deferred pending push authorization, not pushed this session, same pattern as issues #11 and #12.

Issue #13 (Section-index staleness invalidation, Epic Phase 2b) complete -- its one and only subtask, 2b.4.1, implemented, genuinely fix-cycled (CHANGES_REQUESTED -> fix -> PASS re-verification) for a real split-vs-ReadPartial atomicity race, and independently verified. Issue #13 closable; closure deferred pending push authorization, same pattern as issues #11 and #12 (.cdr/commits/task-2b.2-issue-11.md, .cdr/commits/task-2b.3-issue-12.md).

TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace; real production bug found+fixed (ExecuteSplitAtomic/RecoverSplitCommits never created catalog records for split-off fileIDs). Never in dispute across issue 14 fix cycle.

TestReaderDuringSplit; first version was a tautology (no shared mutable state with real split), caught by verification, reworked to share catalog.ContentStore state. Two non-blocking follow-ups tracked in pending.md.

Issue #14 (final issue of Epic Phase 2b, auto-split). Both subtasks verified. Closes issue 14 and completes Epic Phase 2b (issues 10-14) in full, verified+committed locally; GitHub closure deferred pending push authorization.

First subtask under task-3.1 (issue #15, Epic Phase 3). Adds engine/graph/csr.go: CSR (compressed sparse row) compact adjacency-array format for graph.dat -- 28-byte header (magic/version/counts/CRC32), sorted node-ID array, CSR offsets array, flat fixed-width edge array. WriteCSR atomic temp+fsync+rename mirroring catalog/content.go precedent; LoadCSR validates magic/version/CRC, rejects truncated/corrupted files. 5 new tests (round-trip, empty-graph, corruption, truncated-header, large-adjacency). docs/LLD/graph.md updated. Zero changes to existing edge_append.go log. VERIFIED (2026-07-08-002-verification): PASS_WITH_COMMENTS, zero blocking findings. Full-suite engine/split.TestReaderDuringSplit failure reconfirmed pre-existing disclosed flake (already tracked pending.md/regression.jsonl), not a regression -- confirmed via go list -deps showing no engine/graph -> engine/split dependency. Two non-blocking findings tracked in .cdr/memory/pending.md: (1) LoadCSR/decodeCSREdge don't validate on-disk EdgeType byte against known values; (2) pre-existing TestReaderDuringSplit flake reproduced once as expected, no new entry needed. Issue #15 not closable yet: 5 more subtasks (3.1.2-3.1.6) remain. Commit a4e473d local-only, not pushed.

fix_history.bug: F1: compaction retry after failed truncation re-summed already-merged edge-log entries, compounding EdgeEntityCooccur weight (3->6->9...) on repeated retries.

F2: F1's fix caused silent PERMANENT data loss on the ordinary happy path (no crash needed) -- TruncateNode's directory delete restarted WAL segment numbering, colliding with the stale sidecar, silently dropping the next appended edge forever. Found by verifier's own exploratory testing beyond the given test spec.

notes: Periodic compaction of per-node edge log (3.1.2) into CSR graph.dat (3.1.1), issue #15 Epic Phase 3. Unusually long fix cycle: 2 confirmed bugs found+fixed across 3 verification passes (008/010/012-verification). F1 (retry double-counting of ENTITY_COOCCUR weight) fixed via durable per-node graph.dat.compact-state sidecar + EdgeLog.ReadNodeAfter skip-logic. F2 (silent permanent data loss on the happy path, caused by F1's own fix) fixed via wal.WriteSegmentFloor -- durable monotonic per-node segment-number floor, never reused after truncation. Final verification 012-verification PASS_WITH_COMMENTS, zero blocking findings, all revert-experiments independently redone via git worktree. One non-blocking pre-existing lock-ordering gap (EdgeLog.ReadNodeAfter/TruncateNode race, present since ebbc1ff, not introduced by this fix cycle) carried forward in pending.md with forward-reference to GitHub milestone #10 Phase 4.5 (issues #38-42). Issue #15 stays open: 3 subtasks remain (3.1.4-3.1.6). All commits local-only, not pushed.

Full edge-type validation (ENTITY_COOCCUR/LLM_ASSERTED/SPLIT_SIBLING/REDIRECT), issue #15 Epic Phase 3, deferred from 3.1.3. New engine/graph/edge.go: ValidEdgeType, EdgeTypeName/ParseEdgeType canonical string mapping, validated NewCSREdge constructor. Wired into edgelog.go's AppendEdge (rejects any undefined type byte, not just 0) and csr.go's decodeCSREdge/WriteCSR (symmetric decode+encode validation). edge_append.go's SPLIT_SIBLING/REDIRECT-only scoping and compact.go's mergeEdges weight-aggregation confirmed already correct and left unchanged by both implementer and independent verifier. Verification: thorough invalid-byte coverage (0/mid-range/high) across every entry point, zero regressions, full module suite green, no split flake this run. One non-blocking provenance-hygiene finding (014-implementation's handoff.json/metadata.json had a stale commit hash) corrected as part of this close-out. Issue #15 stays open: 2 subtasks remain (3.1.5-3.1.6). Commit 4b9c639 local-only, not pushed.

GraphNeighbors BFS traversal API (0-2 hop) over compacted CSRGraph, issue #15 Epic Phase 3. New engine/graph/traverse.go + traverse_test.go: type-filter prunes traversal itself (disclosed judgment call, LLD silent), dedup by first-seen hop, sort (hop asc, Weight desc, Target asc) then cap so best candidates survive truncation, read-only (no CSRGraph mutation), strict input validation. 14 subtests including issue's literal >maxNodes-reachable-within-2-hops scenario. Zero regressions, full suite green. One low-severity finding fixed directly as part of this close-out: traverse.go's doc comment incorrectly claimed the LLD's Traversal API section (line 111) sits under Storage layout (line 15) -- actually a sibling top-level section separated by an intervening Edge shape section (line 97); underlying design conclusion (EdgeLog never mentioned there) unchanged, only the structural claim was wrong. Fixed in doc-only follow-up commit 369fd65 (matches 3.1.2's metadata-only follow-up commit precedent), re-verified via build/vet/gofmt/targeted test, all clean. Issue #15 stays open: 1 subtask remains (3.1.6, the final one). Commits e837f1b and 369fd65 local-only, not pushed.
